title: Define the external artifact repository contract
objective: Define and validate the repository structure, Markdown format and metadata contract for storing artifacts in a separate private GitHub repository.
userContext: The application currently expects artifacts to exist on a local filesystem, which is not available in the deployed Cloudflare Worker. Artifacts must instead live in a dedicated private GitHub repository that remains human-editable, version-controlled and independent from the application source code.
currentBehaviour: Artifacts are stored under the application repository's local artifacts directory and loaded through filesystem APIs. The existing front-matter schema supports IDs, titles, types, statuses, tags, aliases, source IDs and creation timestamps, but there is no formal contract for an external storage repository.
requiredBehaviour: The project must define a stable, documented storage contract for a dedicated artifact repository. The contract must specify the directory structure, supported Markdown file format, required and optional front-matter fields, allowed values, ID uniqueness rules, nested-directory behaviour, authoritative branch and validation expectations.
userExperience: A maintainer should be able to create or edit artifact Markdown files directly in the storage repository and run a validation command that clearly reports malformed files, duplicate IDs, unsupported values and structural problems.
functionalRequirements: Document the expected directory structure for prompts, agents, snippets, templates, app ideas and variations.
Define the canonical Markdown front-matter schema.
Define required and optional metadata fields.
Define permitted artifact types and statuses.
Define whether artifact IDs must be globally unique across all directories.
Define whether nested directories are supported.
Define the authoritative branch and configurable artifact root path.
Add a reusable validator capable of validating a complete external artifact repository checkout.
Detect malformed front matter, duplicate IDs, missing required fields and unsupported values.
Provide representative valid and invalid fixtures or examples.
Document how the existing sample artifacts should be migrated to the storage repository.
technicalConsiderations: Reuse the existing Zod artifact schema where practical.
Avoid coupling validation to Cloudflare or GitHub authentication.
Keep the storage format human-readable and editable through GitHub.
Prefer Markdown with YAML front matter.
Ensure the schema can support future read and write operations without breaking current artifact content.
outOfScope: Reading the private repository from production.
Moving real private content during automated tests.
acceptanceCriteria: The external artifact repository structure is documented.
The complete artifact metadata schema is documented.
The validator detects malformed files and duplicate artifact IDs.
Valid nested Markdown artifacts can be validated successfully.
Validation errors identify the affected file and reason.
Examples exist for each supported artifact type.
The current application specification is reviewed and updated where the storage contract affects expected behaviour.
Relevant tests, type checking and build validation pass.
The implementation pull request includes DATA-001 and closes the generated issue.
codexGuidance: Review lib/artifact-repository.ts and the existing sample artifacts before implementation. Treat this feature as the canonical data-contract foundation for later GitHub authentication and repository access features. Do not implement GitHub API access.
